summary.intent: Upgrade Go toolchain to 1.26.2 and restructure the gitsync library by moving it from /pkg/gitsync to the repo root, then rename the Go module path from entire.io/entire/git-sync to entire.io/entire/gitsync.
summary.outcome: Go upgraded to 1.26.2, package files flattened to repo root (with unstable/ and internalbridge/ as siblings), pkg/ directory removed, and all import paths updated to entire.io/entire/gitsync. Build, tests, and lint all pass.

repo: Module path was entire.io/entire/git-sync, now entire.io/entire/gitsync — any external consumers would need to update their imports.
The repo uses mise for toolchain management (Go version and golangci-lint pinned in mise.toml).
golangci-lint v2.11.4 was already at the latest release at time of session.
Subpackages unstable/ and internalbridge/ live alongside root-level package files rather than nested under a parent package directory.
Binary entrypoint remains at cmd/git-sync — the CLI binary name was intentionally not renamed.

code.finding: Module renamed from entire.io/entire/git-sync to entire.io/entire/gitsync; Go directive bumped from 1.25.2 to 1.26.2.
Root-level file, previously pkg/gitsync/client.go; package name remains gitsync.
Previously pkg/gitsync/internalbridge/config.go; moved to repo root sibling.
Previously pkg/gitsync/unstable/client.go; moved to repo root sibling.

workflow: Renaming package to 'sync' was explicitly rejected because it shadows Go's stdlib sync package, forcing callers to alias one of the two.
When bulk-replacing import paths in a repo, apply longest-match substitutions first to avoid partial replacements in prose and nested paths.
Git tracks directory moves as renames when files are moved atomically, preserving history.

friction: Mid-task plan change: user interrupted the /pkg/gitsync → /sync move to redirect toward flattening everything to the repo root instead.
Package naming required a three-option analysis before proceeding due to the stdlib 'sync' collision risk.
Two-phase rename (directory restructure + module path rename) required separate bulk-replace passes to avoid leaving stale references.
open_items: cmd/git-sync binary directory and CLI name were intentionally left unrenamed — user was offered the option to rename to 'gitsync' but did not confirm.
Any external consumers importing entire.io/entire/git-sync will be broken and need to update their import paths manually.
docs/embedding.md was updated but should be reviewed to confirm all prose references to the old path and CLI name are accurate.
